findings.title: Identical example user prompt 'Analyze this codebase' shared by 4 agents
findings.description: The user prompt "Analyze this codebase" appears as an example in 4 different agents: code-analyzer, plan-critic, report-critic, and report-reconciler. Per agent-conventions.md, each agent's examples should demonstrate distinct triggering scenarios. Shared prompts create ambiguity about which agent should activate.
findings.recommendation: Use distinct, agent-specific user prompts in examples. For plan-critic: "Validate this refactoring plan". For report-critic: "Check the analysis report quality". For report-reconciler: "Reconcile all dimension findings".

findings.title: Non-standard 'templates/' directory at plugin root
findings.description: The templates/ directory at the plugin root is not part of the canonical plugin layout (skills/, agents/, hooks/, scripts/, references/). While report-reconciler and refactoring-planner agents reference templates/ in their AGENT.md bodies, no SKILL.md mentions this directory, meaning skills cannot discover these templates. Official plugins store templates in skill-level assets/ or references/ subdirectories.
findings.recommendation: Move template files into the relevant skill's assets/ or references/ subdirectory. For example, analysis-draft.md could live in skills/reconcile-report/assets/. Ensure each relocated template is referenced in its parent SKILL.md.

findings.title: Agents 'code-analyzer' and 'report-critic' share color 'cyan'
findings.description: Both code-analyzer and report-critic agents use color: cyan. Per agent-conventions.md section 3.4, agents within the same plugin should use distinct colors to aid visual identification in the Claude Code UI. code-analyzer performs dimension scanning (analysis) while report-critic performs validation — these are semantically different roles that warrant distinct colors.
findings.recommendation: Change report-critic to color: yellow (validation/quality checks semantic) to distinguish it from code-analyzer (cyan for analysis). This aligns with the color semantics table where yellow represents validation and caution.

findings.title: Moderate trigger overlap: 'critique-plan' and 'critique-report' (0.47)
findings.description: Skills critique-plan and critique-report share approximately 47% token overlap in their descriptions (Jaccard ~0.47, below the 0.50 threshold for info findings). Both use terms like 'validate', 'critique', 'quality', 'evaluating'. While they target different artifacts (plan vs report), the overlap may contribute to activation ambiguity.
findings.recommendation: Differentiate descriptions further by emphasizing distinct trigger phrases. critique-plan should emphasize dependency ordering, phase sequencing. critique-report should emphasize scoring accuracy, finding coverage.

findings.title: Moderate description overlap: agents 'plan-critic' and 'report-critic' (0.50)
findings.description: Agents plan-critic and report-critic share approximately 50% token overlap in their descriptions (Jaccard ~0.50). Both validate quality and return structured feedback. While they target different artifacts, the overlapping language reduces trigger differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Make agent descriptions more distinct. plan-critic: focus on dependency correctness, phase ordering, effort realism. report-critic: focus on score arithmetic, finding coverage, recommendation quality.

findings.title: plugin.json contains non-standard field 'peerDependencies'
findings.description: The plugin.json manifest contains a 'peerDependencies' field. This is not part of the official plugin manifest schema (plugin-structure.md section 2). While it does not cause errors, it is not validated or consumed by Claude Code.
findings.recommendation: Document plugin dependencies in README.md instead, or remove the field if the dependency is not enforced at runtime.

findings.title: plugin.json description exceeds 200 character recommendation
findings.snippet: "description": "Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions (structure, quality, security, testing) — produces scored reports with critic validation and focused refactoring plans. v0.8.0 adds token optimizations (split schemas, skip-clean ralph-loop passes, inline scoring, smart model defaults)."
findings.description: The plugin.json description field is 297 characters, exceeding the 50-200 character recommended range per plugin-structure.md section 2.3. The description includes version-specific changelog content ('v0.8.0 adds token optimizations...') which should be in README.md or CHANGELOG.md instead.
findings.recommendation: Shorten the description to under 200 characters by removing the version-specific changelog. Example: "Comprehensive codebase analysis across 4 standard dimensions with scoring, critic validation, and refactoring plans".

findings.title: Non-standard 'docs/' directory at plugin root (development-only)
findings.description: The docs/ directory contains superpowers plans and specs. These are development artifacts not part of the plugin runtime. While they do not cause issues, they are not part of the canonical plugin layout and no SKILL.md references this directory.
findings.recommendation: Consider excluding docs/ from published plugin distributions, or document it as a development-only directory in README.md. No action required if the plugin is not distributed as a package.

findings.title: Plugin has no hooks -- 4 of 14 official plugins use hooks
findings.description: The plugin does not define any hooks. 4 of 14 official plugins use hooks (ralph-loop, hookify, superpowers, plus one other). Hooks can enhance plugin functionality through event-driven automation (e.g., SessionStart for context loading). This is informational only -- hooks are optional.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding SessionStart hooks for context loading if the plugin benefits from automatic environment setup. No action required.
